hypothesis.mechanism: A single all-or-nothing equality / switch CMP tests a contiguous opaque literal of width N bytes (a FOURCC table tag, PNG chunk type, URL scheme, the UTF-8 BOM, an enum/color_type byte, or 'xmlns'); satisfying it routes execution into a specific handler or case arm. cmplog and value_profile_cmplog both carry I2SRandReplace, which reads the literal out of the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes it into the input at the matching offset in one mutation step, so both has_axis fuzzers clear the gate regardless of width. naive has no CMP introspection and must brute-force the N-byte literal by random havoc (~1/256^N). value_profile only adds a Hamming CMP_MAP gradient, which is flat against an opaque equality (every wrong value is equidistant from the match), so it tracks naive rather than the has_axis cluster — the cluster's defining divergence is precisely that value_profile does NOT rescue the gap.
hypothesis.program_feature_parameter: GATE_BYTES (compile-time -D flag) — the width in bytes of the opaque literal the single equality gate compares against; controls the size of the search space the lacks_axis arms must brute-force.
hypothesis.predicted_direction: has_axis fuzzers (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) outperform lacks_axis fuzzers (naive, value_profile); the crash-count ratio grows with GATE_BYTES

predicted_endpoints.GATE_BYTES=1: All four fuzzers crash quickly — a 1-byte literal is ~1/256, reachable by blind havoc within 600s, so has_axis and lacks_axis are at parity.
predicted_endpoints.GATE_BYTES=8: has_axis cluster (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) still crashes effectively instantly via I2S substitution; lacks_axis cluster (naive, value_profile) records 0 crashes — 1/256^8 is intractable and the opaque equality gives value_profile no usable gradient.

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.cmplog: flat-high (resolves at every GATE_BYTES via I2S substitution)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile_cmplog: flat-high (also has I2SRandReplace; may sit at or above cmplog from added VP feedback, but never below)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile: monotone-decreasing, tracking naive (flat Hamming gradient on an opaque equality gives no climb advantage)

hypothesis.tradeoff_observation: value_profile's placement in lacks_axis is the load-bearing, falsifiable claim: although value_profile has CMP_MAP feedback, the gate is an opaque exact equality, so the per-byte Hamming gradient is flat and value_profile behaves like naive, not like the cmplog arm. If the sweep instead shows value_profile resolving at parity with value_profile_cmplog at GATE_BYTES>=4, the I2S-substitution attribution is refuted (this is the union of the members' would_be_refuted_by harness blueprints).

verification.dockerfile: one per scan value, see deployed_targets
